edge_hypothesis: The Supertrend champion (PF=1.921) uses 1.5x ATR stop / 12.0x ATR TP (8:1 R:R). But what if the champion is LEAVING MONEY ON THE TABLE by stopping out trades prematurely? The ULTRA-WIDE STOP thesis: Supertrend's direction flip catches the START of a trend. Some trades initially dip against the entry (market noise, liquidity sweeps, intraday volatility) before the trend resumes. At 1.5x stop, these trades are killed. At 2.0x stop, they SURVIVE the dip and eventually reach the TP. The tradeoff: wider stops mean losing trades lose MORE per trade. But at extreme R:R (14:1-16:1), each winner produces 28-32x ATR profit, dwarfing the 2.0x ATR loss. The MATH: if a 2.0x stop rescues just 2-3% of stopped-out trades (converting them from losers to 14:1 winners), the additional profit per rescued trade (28x ATR) far exceeds the additional loss per failed trade (0.5x ATR extra). Expected rescue rate needed: >1.5% of trades to be net positive. THREE OUTCOMES: (1) PF > 2.0 at 2.0x/16:1 = Supertrend's edge is being THROTTLED by tight stops — major portfolio improvement. (2) PF 1.5-1.9 at 2.0x/14:1 = wider stops help marginally but don't transform the strategy — stick with champion's 1.5x. (3) PF < 1.3 at 2.0x = wider stops let bad trades bleed longer, erasing the rescue benefit — tight stops are structurally important for Supertrend. This spec also tests 1h at 14:1 alongside the champion's 1h baseline, creating a direct comparison with the ONLY variable being stop width and R:R. Regime assumption: ALL (matching champion). Invalidation: If PF < 1.2 across all variants, tight stops ARE the mechanism — Supertrend's edge comes from fast loss-cutting, not trend-riding.

variants.name: supertrend_ultrawide_1h_14to1
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/1h with ULTRA-WIDE stop: 2.0x ATR stop / 28.0x ATR TP (14:1 R:R). The flagship ultra-wide variant. 2.0x ATR stop is 33% wider than the champion's 1.5x — this gives each trade an additional 0.5x ATR of breathing room. On ETH/1h, that's roughly $15-30 more room before stop is hit. The TP at 28.0x ATR targets the FULL EXTENT of multi-day ETH trends (4-8% moves on 1h). Breakeven win rate: 6.7%. With 85+ expected trades, even a 7-8% win rate (6-7 winning trades) produces measurable PF. The diagnostic question: how many of the champion's 80% losing trades would have SURVIVED at 2.0x stop and eventually hit 14:1 TP? If the answer is >5%, this variant will significantly outperform.

variants.name: supertrend_ultrawide_4h_16to1
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/4h with ULTRA-WIDE stop: 2.0x ATR stop / 32.0x ATR TP (16:1 R:R). The EXTREME variant on the higher timeframe. On 4h, 32x ATR TP targets 8-15% ETH moves — these are the multi-week trends that produce 2-3x the average daily range. Supertrend flips on 4h are infrequent (20-40 expected trades) but each correct flip catches a major trend leg. At 16:1 R:R, breakeven is 5.9% win rate — essentially 1 in 17 trades must be a winner. Given that 4h Supertrend flips in trending regimes are high-quality (the champion proves this), even a small number of trend-catching trades at 32x ATR produces significant absolute returns. The risk: with only 20-40 trades, statistical significance is limited. But if 3-4 trades hit TP, PF will be substantial.

variants.name: supertrend_ultrawide_1h_12to1_compare
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/1h with 2.0x ATR stop / 24.0x ATR TP (12:1 R:R). The CONTROLLED COMPARISON variant. The champion has 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP = 8:1 R:R. This variant has 2.0x stop / 24.0x TP = 12:1 R:R. BOTH share 1h/ETH/Supertrend. The comparison isolates BOTH variables simultaneously: wider stop AND higher R:R. If PF is similar (~1.9), the wider stop's rescue benefit compensates for the fewer-winning-trades at 12:1 (vs 8:1). If PF is higher (~2.2+), the combination is synergistic — wider stops rescue trades that then have room to reach the higher TP. If PF is lower (~1.3-1.5), the champion's 1.5x/8:1 combination is locally optimal. Breakeven: 7.7% win rate. Expected: 85 trades.
